希伯伦袭击现场 以色列军方说，其中两人在杰宁向以色列士兵开枪，另外一人在耶路撒冷试图持刀袭击以色列警察。 过去几个月，巴以局势恶化，接连发生袭击、暴力冲突事件。 自从2015年10月起，已经有160名巴勒斯坦人、26名以色列人丧生。 周日（14日）发生的第一起事件当中，以色列军方说，两名男少年在西岸北部的杰宁附近向路经的车辆投掷石块。 军方表示，在巡逻车抵达现场后，其中一名少年开枪。 士兵当即将这两名巴勒斯坦少年击毙。据报，这两人都年仅15岁。 此后在一处检查站点，以色列警方说，一名巴勒斯坦人持刀冲向正在检查进入耶路撒冷车辆的警官。警官开枪将其打死。 据报这名少年今年17岁。 记者说，目前暴力事件仍然没有减少迹象。虽然时好时坏，但是持续发生。 （编译/责编：苏平）

instruction: 希伯伦袭击现场 以色列军方说，其中两人在杰宁向以色列士兵开枪，另外一人在耶路撒冷试图持刀袭击以色列警察。 过去几个月，巴以局势恶化，接连发生袭击、暴力冲突事件。 自从2015年10月起，已经有160名巴勒斯坦人、26名以色列人丧生。 周日（14日）发生的第一起事件当中，以色列军方说，两名男少年在西岸北部的杰宁附近向路经的车辆投掷石块。 军方表示，在巡逻车抵达现场后，其中一名少年开枪。 士兵当即将这两名巴勒斯坦少年击毙。据报，这两人都年仅15岁。 此后在一处检查站点，以色列警方说，一名巴勒斯坦人持刀冲向正在检查进入耶路撒冷车辆的警官。警官开枪将其打死。 据报这名少年今年17岁。 记者说，目前暴力事件仍然没有减少迹象。虽然时好时坏，但是持续发生。 （编译/责编：苏平）

Write a summary of the wikipedia page "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raymond_III,_Count_of_Tripoli".

instruction: Write a summary of the wikipedia page "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raymond_III,_Count_of_Tripoli".

content.text:
*Raymond III Count of Tripoli* was a significant crusader leader of the twelfth century. He was born around 1140 and inherited the county of Tripoli after the death of his father Raymond II. His early years as count were defined by military misfortune and subsequent captivity. In 1164 he was taken prisoner at the Battle of Harim by forces loyal to Nur ad-Din the Zengid ruler of Syria. He remained a captive for approximately ten years before being ransomed and returning to lead his county. During this period of absence his lands were administered by regents who managed to preserve the county against various external threats. His experience of captivity profoundly shaped his later political outlook and his preference for diplomacy and negotiated peace over aggressive military action.

*Political Career and Regency* brought Raymond to prominence beyond the boundaries of his own county. He served as regent of the Kingdom of Jerusalem on two separate occasions. The first was during the final years of Baldwin IV's reign and the second was during the minority of the young king Baldwin V. As regent he was responsible for managing the defence and governance of the kingdom at a particularly vulnerable moment in its history. He pursued a policy of truce with Saladin the Ayyubid sultan who had gradually consolidated control over the surrounding territories. This diplomatic approach was deeply unpopular with more aggressive barons who felt that any accommodation with Muslim rulers was a betrayal of crusader principles. His rival Raynald of Chatillon repeatedly violated agreements and provoked incidents that undermined Raymond's strategy of cautious coexistence.

*Legacy and Death* followed in the wake of the catastrophic defeat at the Battle of Hattin in July 1187. Raymond's cavalry managed to break through the encircling Saracen lines and escape but the rest of the crusader army was destroyed and King Guy was captured. The True Cross was also lost in the battle. Raymond died later that same year at Tripoli under circumstances that are not entirely clear in the historical record. Scholars continue to debate his legacy: some regard him as a realist who correctly understood that the crusader states lacked the resources for sustained offensive warfare while others argue that his accommodation with Saladin fatally weakened crusader solidarity and contributed to the disaster at Hattin.

input.content: Your response should not contain any commas. Your response should highlight at least 3 sections that have titles in markdown format, for example *highlighted section part 1*, *highlighted section part 2*, *highlighted section part 3*. Your response should be 300 or more words in length.
Write a summary of the wikipedia page "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raymond_III,_Count_of_Tripoli".

Raymond III (1140–1187) was the Count of Tripoli from 1163 until his death. He was the son of Raymond II Count of Tripoli and Hodierna of Jerusalem. *highlighted section part 1*: Raymond III inherited the County of Tripoli upon the death of his father in 1153 but he was still a minor at the time. His mother Hodierna acted as regent until he came of age in 1163.

*highlighted section part 2*: In 1164 Raymond III married Eschiva of Bures who was the heiress of Bures-sur-Yvette. Their marriage produced two sons: Raymond (who predeceased his father) and Bohemond IV who succeeded Raymond III as Count of Tripoli. In 1171 Raymond III supported the claim of his first cousin Amalric to the Kingdom of Jerusalem against Nur ad-Din Zangi the Sultan of Aleppo and Damascus. However Nur ad-Din defeated the Crusader army at the Battle of Harrim.

*highlighted section part 3*: In 1186 Raymond III joined forces with Guy of Lusignan the King of Jerusalem to attack Saladin the Sultan of Egypt and Syria. This campaign ended in disaster for the Crusaders when Saladin's forces defeated them at the Battle of Hattin in 1187. Raymond III was among those captured by Saladin's troops. He died in captivity later that year. The County of Tripoli eventually fell to Saladin's forces in 1289 marking the end of the state that Raymond III had once ruled.

Raymond III's reign saw increased hostilities between the Crusader states and the growing power of Nur ad-Din Zangi and later Saladin. Although he allied with the Kingdom of Jerusalem Raymond III was unable to stop the Muslim conquest of Tripoli and the other Crusader territories. His death in captivity after the Battle of Hattin marked the downfall of Crusader authority in the region.
Describe Raymond III's military achievements, but without mentioning territory losses.

instruction: Describe Raymond III's military achievements, but without mentioning territory losses.

content.text:
*Military Campaigns* defined much of Raymond III's career as Count of Tripoli. His first major military engagement ended in disaster at the Battle of Harim in 1164 where he was captured by Nur ad-Din's forces and held for nearly a decade. Upon his release he returned to active leadership and worked to stabilize his county through negotiated agreements rather than open warfare. As regent of the Kingdom of Jerusalem he organized the kingdom's defensive posture during a period when Saladin was rapidly consolidating his hold on surrounding territories. He understood that the crusader states were militarily outmatched in any prolonged conflict and that their survival depended on careful management of resources and alliances rather than on aggressive campaigning.

*Strategic Thinking* shaped every major military decision throughout his career. He sought to avoid pitched battles wherever possible and preferred to maintain the kingdom's fighting strength through defensive deployments and truces. His arrangement of a truce with Saladin during the regency was a calculated measure intended to buy time and preserve manpower. This strategy was undermined when Raynald of Chatillon attacked a Saracen caravan and provoked a full-scale invasion. Raymond attempted to arrange a separate local agreement with Saladin to protect his own lands but was ultimately unable to prevent the broader confrontation that culminated at Hattin.

*At the Battle of Hattin* in 1187 Raymond led the advance guard of the crusader army. When the Saracen encirclement tightened he led his cavalry in a charge that broke through the opposing lines and allowed his forces to escape. The remainder of the army was overwhelmed and destroyed and King Guy was taken prisoner. Whether Raymond's escape represented sound tactical judgment or something more questionable has been debated by historians ever since. He died at Tripoli shortly after Hattin without witnessing the fall of Jerusalem that followed in the autumn of that same year.

Upon his release he returned to active leadership and worked to stabilize his county through negotiated agreements rather than open warfare.
